title: castilleja arvensis var. pastorei
intro: Castilleja arvensis var. pastorei, also known as Indian paintbrush, is a perennial plant species in the family Orobanchaceae. This hemiparasitic plant is known for its unique, red and yellow, brush-like flowers and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant contains alkaloids, including ajadilactone, which have been reported to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, offering potential health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and soothing digestive issues. Castilleja arvensis var. pastorei can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its purported health benefits, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
